revision.comment: Bot: remove [[Category:Sn crud]] (crud category cleanup) [[github:EmmaLeonhart/shintowiki-scripts/actions/runs/23985667504|commit triggered pipeline]]
revision.text:
[[ja:日高神社]] {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | Name = Hitaka Shrine (日高神社) | image = File:日高神社.JPG | location = {{ill|Mizusawa|en|Mizusawa|qid=Q1114302|lt=Mizusawa, Ōshū, Iwate|1=Mizusawa}}, {{ill|Ōshū, Iwate|en|Ōshū, Iwate|qid=Q386052|lt=Ōshū, Iwate|1=Ōshū, Iwate}}, {{ill|Iwate Prefecture|en|Iwate Prefecture|qid=Q48326|lt=Iwate Prefecture|1=Iwate Prefecture}}, 13 Hidaka Kōji | coordinates = {{Coord|39.142889|N|141.132361|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deity = {{ill|Ame-no-Minakanushi|qid=Q2842779|1=Ame-no-Minakanushi}} | Shrine Rank = Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|qid=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|qid=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q712534}} | Established = Traditionally 1st year of {{ill|Kōnin (era)|en|Kōnin (era)|qid=Q1373070|lt=Kōnin era|1=Kōnin (era)}} (810) | Main Hall = Three-bay Nagare-zukuri style, copper roof | Other Names = Hitaka Myōken | Pilgrimage Route = | Annual Festival = April 22 | Rituals = {{ill|Hitaka Hibuse Festival|ja|日高火防祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hitaka Hibuse Festival|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 日高火防祭り|1=Hitaka Hibuse Festival|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11510844}} (April 28-29) | Map = {{location map|Japan Iwate|lat_deg=39|lat_min=08|lat_sec=34.4|lon_deg=141|lon_min=07|lon_sec=56.5|label=Hitaka Shrine|position=center|width=200|float=center|caption=}} | location = {{ill|Oshu|qid=Q386052|1=Ōshū, Iwate}} }} '''Hitaka Shrine''' (日高神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|qid=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Mizusawa|en|Mizusawa|qid=Q1114302|lt=Mizusawa, Ōshū, Iwate|1=Mizusawa}}, {{ill|Ōshū, Iwate|en|Ōshū, Iwate|qid=Q386052|lt=Ōshū, Iwate|1=Ōshū, Iwate}}, {{ill|Iwate Prefecture|en|Iwate Prefecture|qid=Q48326|lt=Iwate Prefecture|1=Iwate Prefecture}}. It was classified as a former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|qid=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|qid=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q712534}}. The shrine is known for its {{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|en|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|qid=Q1188622|lt=Important Cultural Property|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)}}-designated main hall and the {{ill|Hitaka Hibuse Festival|ja|日高火防祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hitaka Hibuse Festival|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 日高火防祭り|1=Hitaka Hibuse Festival|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11510844}}. == Name == [[File:日高神社参道.JPG|thumb|right|200px|The sacred forest and the approach to Hitaka Shrine]] The origin of the shrine's name has multiple theories. One suggests that the area was historically known as {{ill|Hitakami|en|Hitakami|qid=Q11288931|lt=Hitakami no Kuni|1=Hitakami}} and served as a central location for its people. Another tradition states that during the {{ill|Former Nine Years' War|en|Former Nine Years' War|qid=Q1187448|lt=Former Nine Years' War|1=Former Nine Years' War}}, {{ill|Minamoto no Yoriyoshi|en|Minamoto no Yoriyoshi|qid=Q3082851|lt=Minamoto no Yoriyoshi|1=Minamoto no Yoriyoshi}} prayed at this shrine, and soon after, the rain ceased, the sun rose high in the sky, and by the Hour of the Goat (around 2 PM), he successfully defeated {{ill|Abe no Sadato|en|Abe no Sadato|qid=Q3616500|lt=Abe no Sadatō|1=Abe no Sadato}}. The shrine is also referred to as "Hitaka Myōken" or "Hitaka Myōken-gū" due to its association with {{ill|Myōken|en|Myōken|qid=Q10960623|lt=Myōken|1=Myōken}}, a Buddhist deity. == Deities == The shrine enshrines the following eight deities: * {{ill|Ame-no-Minakanushi|en|Ame-no-Minakanushi|qid=Q2842779|lt=Ame-no-Minakanushi|1=Ame-no-Minakanushi}} * {{ill|Kagu-tsuchi|ja|火産霊神|1=Kagu-tsuchi|lt=Kagutsuchi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagu-tsuchi|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagutsuchi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to カグツチ}} * {{ill|Toshigami|lt=Ōtoshi-no-Kami|ja|大年神|1=Toshigami|lt=Ōtoshi-no-kami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toshigami|13=User:Immanuelle/Toshigami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 年神}} * {{ill|Mitoshi-no-Kami|ja|御年神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mitoshi-no-Kami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 年神|1=Toshigami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q3028663}} * Wakatoshi-no-Kami * {{ill|Mizuhanome|lt=Mizuha-no-Me|ja|水波能売神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mizuhanome|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ミヅハノメ}} * {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}} * {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|倉稲魂神|1=Ukanomitama|2=ja|3=倉稲魂神|4=de|5=Ukanomitama|6=eo|7=Ukanomitama|8=mni|9=ꯎꯀꯅꯣꯃꯤꯇꯃ|10=ru|11=Ука-но_митама|12=simple|13=Ukanomitama|14=sv|15=Ukanomitama|16=zh|17=宇迦之御魂神|qid=Q3080728|lt=Ukanomitama|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukanomitama|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ウカノミタマ}} Ame-no-Minakanushi is frequently syncretized with {{ill|Myōken|en|Myōken|qid=Q10960623|lt=Myōken|1=Myōken}}. == History == === Founding Legend === According to tradition, Hitaka Shrine was established in the 1st year of the {{ill|Kōnin (era)|en|Kōnin (era)|qid=Q1373070|lt=Kōnin era|1=Kōnin (era)}} (810) by imperial decree of {{ill|Emperor Saga|en|Emperor Saga|qid=Q316654|lt=Emperor Saga|1=Emperor Saga}}. This event occurred eight years after the submission of {{ill|Aterui|en|Aterui|qid=Q1088046|lt=Aterui|1=Aterui}} and the construction of {{ill|Isawa Castle|en|Isawa Castle|qid=Q11611683|lt=Fort Isawa|1=Isawa Castle}} by {{ill|Sakanoue no Tamuramaro|en|Sakanoue no Tamuramaro|qid=Q941979|lt=Sakanoue no Tamuramaro|1=Sakanoue no Tamuramaro}} in 802. It is believed that the shrine was created as part of the central government's efforts to integrate local beliefs with those of the Yamato court. === Heian Period and Beyond === The shrine was visited for battle prayers by {{ill|Minamoto no Yoriyoshi|en|Minamoto no Yoriyoshi|qid=Q3082851|lt=Minamoto no Yoriyoshi|1=Minamoto no Yoriyoshi}} and his son {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshiie|en|Minamoto no Yoshiie|qid=Q32613|lt=Minamoto no Yoshiie|1=Minamoto no Yoshiie}} during both the {{ill|Former Nine Years' War|en|Former Nine Years' War|qid=Q1187448|lt=Former Nine Years' War|1=Former Nine Years' War}} and the {{ill|Gosannen War|en|Gosannen War|qid=Q1187411|lt=Later Three Years' War|1=Gosannen War}}. It was also revered by the {{ill|Izawa clan|ja|伊沢氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izawa clan|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 留守氏|1=Rusu clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q2975345}}, the {{ill|Northern Fujiwara|en|Northern Fujiwara|qid=Q1050942|lt=Northern Fujiwara|1=Northern Fujiwara}}, and later by the {{ill|Date clan|en|Date clan|qid=Q1151061|lt=Date clan|1=Date clan}}. In 1170 (2nd year of {{ill|Kaō (era)|en|Kaō (era)|qid=Q1150842|lt=Kaō era|1=Kaō (era)}}), {{ill|Fujiwara no Hidehira|en|Fujiwara no Hidehira|qid=Q3090625|lt=Fujiwara no Hidehira|1=Fujiwara no Hidehira}} sponsored the reconstruction of the shrine.‘''Rikuchū Province Isawa District Village Records''’ (陸中国胆沢郡村誌, 1880). Notably, this was the same year Hidehira was appointed Chinjufu-shōgun (鎮守府将軍). During the late 16th century, {{ill|Date Masamune|en|Date Masamune|qid=Q311183|lt=Date Masamune|1=Date Masamune}} contributed to its rebuilding and granted shrine lands. In 1632 (9th year of {{ill|Kan'ei|en|Kan'ei|qid=Q1192913|lt=Kan'ei era|1=Kan'ei}}), {{ill|Lusumune Tadayoshi|ja|留守宗利|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lusumune Tadayoshi|1=Date Munetosi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11237935}}, the first lord of {{ill|Mizusawa Castle|ja|水沢城|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mizusawa Castle|1=Mizusawa Castle|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11548846}}, ordered the reconstruction of the main shrine building. === Modern Era === In 1869 (2nd year of {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|qid=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era|1=Meiji era}}), Hitaka Shrine became the general shrine of {{ill|Isawa Prefecture|ja|胆沢県|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Isawa Prefecture|1=Isawa Prefecture|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11611687}}. In 1874, it was designated as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|qid=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|qid=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q712534}} (a significant local shrine). This Ranking system was abolished in 1946. == Shrine Buildings == The main shrine building, constructed in 1632, features a three-bay {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|en|Nagare-zukuri|qid=Q210684|lt=Nagare-zukuri|1=Nagare-zukuri}} style with a copper roof. It was designated an {{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|en|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|qid=Q1188622|lt=Important Cultural Property|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)}} in 1990 for its unique Edo-period architectural features.[http://www.bunka.pref.iwate.jp/ciss/bunkazai/detail/id/33 Iwate Prefecture Cultural Heritage Database - Hitaka Shrine Main Hall] == Cultural Properties == (The date in parentheses indicates the designation date.) '''National Important Cultural Property''' * Main Hall (建造物, September 11, 1990) '''Iwate Prefectural Cultural Properties''' * Zuizan Shrine (祖霊社) (建造物, October 27, 1972) * Hitaka Hibuse Festival Hayashi Music (無形民俗, December 24, 1963) '''Ōshū City Cultural Properties''' * Six Stone Lanterns (有形, April 1, 1974) * Graves of the Lusumune Family (史跡, April 1, 1974) * Two Ubasugi Cedar Trees (天然記念物, March 30, 1957 & April 1, 1972) == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://www.ganshinsei.jp/ousyuushi/372a.html Hitaka Shrine] (Iwate Prefecture Shinto Youth Association) * [https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/102/100 Hitaka Shrine Main Hall] (Agency for Cultural Affairs Database) {{translated page|ja|日高神社|version=103018103|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:日高神社]] [[ja:日高神社 (奥州市)]] [[Category:Pages with duplicated content]] ==Merged second translation== {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Hitaka Shrine | image = [[File:日高神社.JPG|270px|Hitaka Shrine]]
Entrance to Hitaka Shrine grounds {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=270|coord={{coord|39|08|34.4|N|141|07|56.5|E}}}} | location = {{ill|Iwate Prefecture|en|Iwate Prefecture|qid=Q48326|lt=Iwate Prefecture|1=Iwate Prefecture}}, {{ill|Ōshū, Iwate|en|Ōshū, Iwate|qid=Q386052|lt=Ōshū, Iwate|1=Ōshū, Iwate}}, Mizusawa, Hitaka-Kōji 13 | coordinates = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|39|08|34.4|N|141|07|56.5|E|region:JP-03_scale:25000_type:landmark|display=inline,title }} | enshrined_deities = {{ill|Ame-no-Minakanushi|en|Ame-no-Minakanushi|qid=Q2842779|lt=Ame-no-Minakanushi|1=Ame-no-Minakanushi}} and 7 other deities | shrine_Rank = Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|qid=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|qid=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q712534}} | founded = Tradition: Kōnin 1 (810) | main_hall = San'gensha Nagare-zukuri, Copper Roof | alternative_names = Hitaka Myōken | pilgrimage_sites = | annual_festival = April 22 | rituals = Hitaka Hibuse Festival (April 28-29) | map = {{location map|Japan Iwate|lat_deg=39|lat_min=08|lat_sec=34.4|lon_deg=141|lon_min=07|lon_sec=56.5|label=Hitaka Shrine|position=center|width=200|float=center|caption=}} }} '''Hitaka Shrine''' (日高神社, ひたかじんじゃ) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|qid=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Mizusawa, Iwate|en|Mizusawa, Iwate|qid=Q1133151|lt=Mizusawa, Ōshū|1=Mizusawa, Iwate}}, {{ill|Iwate Prefecture|en|Iwate Prefecture|qid=Q48326|lt=Iwate Prefecture|1=Iwate Prefecture}}. It was formerly Ranked as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|qid=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|qid=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q712534}}. The shrine is known for its **Important Cultural Property-designated main hall** and the **Hitaka Hibuse Festival** (日高火防祭). == Name == [[File:日高神社参道.JPG|thumb|right|200px|Hitaka Shrine's sacred forest and the approach along Hitaka Kōji]] There are multiple theories regarding the origin of the shrine's name. Some suggest that the region was historically called **Hitakami Province (日高見国)** and served as the center of power for the **Hitakami forces**. Another tradition attributes the name to **Minamoto no Yoshiie**'s prayer during the **Former Nine Years' War (前九年の役)**, when after his supplication, the rain **su11enly stopped, and the sun rose high in the sky** at the hour of the goat (**around 2 PM**), allowing him to **defeat Abe no Sadatō**. Since the shrine also enshrines **Myōken Bosatsu**, it has been referred to as **Hitaka Myōken** or **Hitaka Myōken-gū**. == Enshrined Deities == The shrine enshrines the following **eight deities**: - {{ill|Ame-no-Minakanushi|ja|天御中主神|1=Ame-no-Minakanushi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Minakanushi|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Minakanushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天之御中主神}} - {{ill|Kagu-tsuchi|lt=Kagu-tsuchi|lt=Kagutsuchi|ja|火産霊神|1=Kagu-tsuchi|lt=Kagutsuchi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagutsuchi|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagutsuchi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to カグツチ}} - {{ill|Toshigami|ja|大年神|1=Toshigami|lt=Ōtoshi-no-kami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toshigami|13=User:Immanuelle/Toshigami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 年神}} - {{ill|Otoshi-no-Kami|ja|御年神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otoshi-no-Kami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 年神|1=Toshigami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q3028663}} - Wakatoshi-no-Kami (若年神) - {{ill|Mizuhanome-no-Kami|ja|水波能売神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mizuhanome-no-Kami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ミヅハノメ|1=Mizuhanome|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q8191796}} - {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}} - {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|倉稲魂神|1=Ukanomitama|2=ja|3=倉稲魂神|4=de|5=Ukanomitama|6=eo|7=Ukanomitama|8=mni|9=ꯎꯀꯅꯣꯃꯤꯇꯃ|10=ru|11=Ука-но_митама|12=simple|13=Ukanomitama|14=sv|15=Ukanomitama|16=zh|17=宇迦之御魂神|qid=Q3080728|lt=Ukanomitama|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukanomitama|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ウカノミタマ}} Ame-no-Minakanushi is **often syncretized** with **Myōken Bosatsu**. == History == === Foundation === According to tradition, **Hitaka Shrine** was founded in **Kōnin 1 (810)** under an **imperial edict** from **Emperor Saga**. This occurred **eight years** after the **surrender of Aterui**, the local chief, and the establishment of **Fort Taga (胆沢城)** in **Enryaku 21 (802)** by **Sakanoue no Tamuramaro**. The shrine was likely built as part of an effort to **integrate local religious traditions** with the **central government's deities**, making it the **guardian shrine of the Taga Chinjufu (胆沢鎮守府, the regional military government)**. === Development in the Heian Period and Beyond === During the **Former Nine Years' War (前九年の役)** and **Later Three Years' War (後三年の役)**, **Minamoto no Yoriyoshi** and **Minamoto no Yoshiie** prayed at the shrine for victory. The shrine was also venerated by **Tohoku governors**, including the **Izawa clan** (later known as the **Rusu clan**), the **Ōshū Fujiwara clan**, and the **Date clan**. In **Kaō 2 (1170)**, **Fujiwara no Hidehira** rebuilt the shrine.*Rikuchū-koku Isawa-gun Sonshi* (陸中国胆沢郡村誌, 1880). This was also the year Hidehira was appointed **Chinjufu Shōgun (鎮守府将軍)**. During the **Keichō era** (late 16th century), **Date Masamune** restored the shrine, donating land and constructing new buildings. Records indicate he **personally visited the shrine**. In **Kan'ei 9 (1632)**, the **first lord of Mizusawa Castle, Rusu Munetoshi** (伊達宗利) renovated the current main shrine building. === Modern Era === When **Isawa Prefecture** (胆沢県) was established in **Meiji 2 (1869)**, Hitaka Shrine was designated the **sōsha (総社, regional shrine)** and officially Ranked as a **gōsha** in **Meiji 4 (1871)**. However, the **gōsha Ranking system** was abolished in **Shōwa 21 (1946)**. == Festivals == - **Spring Festival** – **April 22** - **Summer Festival** – **August 22** The **spring festival** was traditionally held on **Lunar New Year’s Day (旧暦正月22日)** and was accompanied by the **Hibuse Festival (火防祭, "Fire Prevention Festival")**, which is now held on the **last Saturday of April**. The **Hibuse Festival** originated when **Rusu Munekage**, lord of Mizusawa Castle, experienced the **Great Meireki Fire** in Edo. Upon returning, he **organized a fire brigade** and offered prayers to **Hitaka Shrine ("Hi" = Fire) and Mizuyama Shrine ("Mizu" = Water)**. The festival features **Hibuse Bayashi (fire prevention music), a form of traditional music designated as an Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Iwate Prefecture**, performed as **festival floats parade through the town**. (See: {{ill|Hitaka Hibuse Festival|ja|日高火防祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hitaka Hibuse Festival|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 日高火防祭り|1=Hitaka Hibuse Festival|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11510844}}) == Shrine Buildings == [[File:日高神社拝殿.JPG|thumb|right|200px|Hitaka Shrine Worship Hall]] - **Main Hall** (本殿): San'gensha Nagare-zukuri, copper roof (formerly thatched). - Rebuilt by **Rusu Munetoshi** in **Kan'ei 9 (1632)**. - Features **elaborate carvings**, a **unique rainbow beam (虹梁)**, and **intricate wooden joinery**, incorporating **Muromachi-period techniques**. - Designated a **National Important Cultural Property** in **Heisei 2 (1990)**. == Cultural Properties == (Year of designation in parentheses) '''National Important Cultural Properties''' - **Main Hall** (建造物, Heisei 2, 1990) '''Iwate Prefectural Cultural Properties''' - **Mizuyama Shrine (祖霊社)** (建造物, Shōwa 47, 1972) - **Hibuse Bayashi (日高囃子, "Hitaka Festival Music")** (無形民俗文化財, Shōwa 38, 1963) '''Ōshū City Cultural Properties''' - **Stone Lanterns (6 units)** (有形文化財, Shōwa 49, 1974) - **Rusu Clan Cemetery** (史跡, Shōwa 49, 1974) - **Two Giant Cedars (姥杉, "Granny Cedars")** (天然記念物, Shōwa 32, 1957 / Shōwa 47, 1972) == External Links == * [http://www.ganshinsei.jp/ousyuushi/372a.html Hitaka Shrine] (Iwate Prefecture Shinto Youth Association) * [http://www.bunka.pref.iwate.jp/rekishi/bunkazai/index.html Iwate Prefectural Cultural Properties Database] * [https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/102/100 Hitaka Shrine Main Hall] (Agency for Cultural Affairs Cultural Property Database) {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Shinto-stub}} '''Spring Matsuri''' (春祭)『{{ill|Daijisen|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daijisen|qq=}} 』Heibonsha 『{{ill|MyPedia|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/MyPedia|qq=}} 』Heibonsha 『{{ill|Sekai Daihyakka Jiten|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sekai Daihyakka Jiten|qq=}} 』, 2nd EditionShogakukan 『{{ill|Nipponica|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nipponica|qq=}} 』, also called '''Spring Festival''' or '''Haru no Matsuri''' (はるまつり), is a general term for {{ill|festivals|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/festivals|qq=}} held in the {{ill|spring|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/spring|qq=}}. == See Also == * '''[[Spring Matsuri]]''' ─ {{ill|Summer Matsuri|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Summer Matsuri|qq=}} ─ {{ill|Autumn Matsuri|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Autumn Matsuri|qq=}} ─ {{ill|Winter Matsuri|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Winter Matsuri|qq=}} * Festivals as seasonal words: '''Spring Matsuri''' ─ Festivals ({{ill|Summer Matsuri|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Summer Matsuri|qq=}}) ─ {{ill|Autumn Matsuri|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Autumn Matsuri|qq=}} * {{ill|Kinensai|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinensai|qq=}} * * {{ill|Easter|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Easter|qq=}} * {{ill|Walpurgis Night|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Walpurgis Night|qq=}} * {{ill|Passover|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Passover|qq=}} * {{ill|The Rite of Spring|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/The Rite of Spring|qq=}} – Ballet music by Igor Stravinsky * {{ill|Flower Festival (Folklore)|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Flower Festival (Folklore)|qq=}} – Andean folklore * {{ill|Reisai|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Reisai|qq=}} * {{ill|Cherry blossom festival|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Cherry blossom festival|qq=}} * {{ill|Maruyama Park Shinkyoku Darake no Spring Matsuri』 * {{ill|Summer Matsuri|lt=Summer Matsuri|lt=Summer festivals|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Summer festivals|qq=}} * {{ill|Autumn Matsuri|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Autumn Festivals|qq=}} * * {{ill|Winter Matsuri|lt=Winter Matsuri|lt=Winter Festivals|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Winter Festivals|qq=}} * {{translated page|ja|日高神社|version=103018103|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:日高神社]] [[ja:日高火防祭り]] [[ja:大國魂神社]] [[ja:くらやみ祭]] [[ja:大辞泉]] [[ja:マイペディア]] [[ja:世界大百科事典]] [[ja:日本大百科全書]] [[ja:祭]] [[ja:春]] [[ja:季語]] [[ja:初春]] [[ja:歳時記]] [[ja:行事]] [[ja:水原秋桜子]] [[ja:石田波郷]] [[ja:前田普羅]] [[ja:祈年祭]] [[ja:夏祭]] [[ja:秋祭]] [[ja:復活祭]] [[ja:無形文化遺産の一覧]] [[ja:ヴァルプルギスの夜]] [[ja:過越]] [[ja:春の祭典]] [[ja:花祭り (フォルクローレ)]] [[ja:festival]] [[ja:桜祭り]] [[ja:丸山公園 (安芸高田市)]] [[ja:太鼓の達人]] [[ja:春祭]] [[ja:春祭り]] [[ja:秋祭り]] [[ja:冬祭り]] [[ja:七夕]] [[ja:地域おこし]] [[ja:帰省]] [[ja:夏休み]] [[zh:%E5%A4%8F%E7%A5%AD]] [[ja:夏祭り]] {{Commons category|Hidaka-jinja}} {{Commons category|Summer festivals in Japan}} [[zh:夏祭]] {{wikidata link|Q11510848}} [[Category:Buildings and structures in Oshu, Iwate]] [[Category:Cultural heritage monuments in Japan]] [[Category:Edo period architecture]] [[Category:Festivals]] [[Category:Festivals in Japan]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Iwate Prefecture]] [[Category:Gō-sha]] [[Category:History of Oshu, Iwate]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Iwate Prefecture]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Iwate Prefecture designated tangible cultural property]] [[Category:Municipal Tangible Cultural Properties in Iwate Prefecture]] [[Category:Pages with duplicated content]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Iwate Prefecture]] [[Category:Spring]] [[Category:Summer]] [[Category:Ward and municipal designated natural monuments]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Pages with 50+ untranslated japanese characters]] [[Category:Pages with 100+ untranslated japanese characters]]
